description: Capability for the SETTINGS window only. Grants just the core APIs the settings UI needs (events to broadcast visibility, window controls for the modal). DELIBERATELY grants NO filesystem plugin permissions — a settings dialog reads/writes its config through Rust IPC commands (which use std::fs directly, bypassing the plugin), so it has no legitimate need for frontend-exposed fs access. This contains the blast radius of a compromised settings page: even if XSSed, it cannot write arbitrary files via the fs plugin.
